# F026. Technical + Factor + XGBoost with CVaR Overlay

## Positioning

This candidate is the more aggressive hybrid: technicals, factors, a boosted tree stack, and a risk overlay that explicitly cares about the left tail. It should only survive if the risk layer can justify the extra complexity.

## Reference Basis

- [LightGBM: A Highly Efficient Gradient Boosting Decision Tree](../../references/papers/lightgbm_neurips_2017.pdf) - tabular baseline for the boosted-tree family
- [Machine Learning Approach to Portfolio Risk](../../references/papers/2_10_Machine_Learning_Approach_to_Portfolio_Risk.pdf) - CVaR and risk-aware allocation framing
- [View fusion vis-à-vis a Bayesian interpretation of Black-Litterman for portfolio allocation](../../references/papers/2301.13594_black_litterman_fusion.pdf) - posterior fusion for blending technical and factor views

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Fundamental Stack

Point-in-time fundamentals and macro-aware feature joins for slower rebalance cycles.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - fundamental and macro data loader
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature joins and lag alignment
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - tabular score model for the hybrid stack

### Cross-Sectional Ranker

The executable ranking path that turns scores into a tradable long/flat or top-k book.
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - LightGBM scoring model
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature matrix construction for the ranker

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Use technicals, factors, and any lagged macro features that can be joined point-in-time.
- Keep the factor library and the feature builder aligned so the boosted tree does not inherit leakage.
- Treat the CVaR overlay as a risk gate, not as a reason to grow the signal.

## Execution Path

- Generate a boosted-tree score and pass it through a CVaR-aware allocation rule.
- Keep the overlay conservative when the left tail widens.
- Use the same backtest engine and paper-safe path to compare against F001 and F012.

## Risk Controls

- Do not let optimization complexity overwhelm the signal.
- Reject any CVaR solution that is numerically unstable or too slow for the rebalance schedule.
- Keep the risk overlay explicit and auditable; this is not a black-box hedge fund trick.

## Validation Gates

- Compare tail loss, turnover, and solve time against the simpler rankers.
- Check whether the CVaR layer actually improves worst-case periods.
- Measure whether the boosted tree contributes anything after the risk overlay is applied.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if the risk overlay adds more instability than protection.
- Archive if the boosted tree does not improve over F001/F012 after costs.
- Archive if the optimization step becomes a maintenance burden.

## Next Step

Use this as the stress-test version of the hybrid stack and only promote it if the tail improves.
